nodes.name: HTTP Request: Exchange Code With Meta
HTTP Request: Subscribe WABA To App
nodes.notes: Uses the business token returned by the previous exchange node. Keep this workflow server-side only.

parameters.query:
insert into connected_whatsapp_accounts (waba_id, phone_number_id, business_id, business_token, token_metadata, owner, status, connected_at, updated_at)
values (
  '{{ $json.waba_id }}',
  '{{ $json.phone_number_id }}',
  {{ $json.business_id ? `'${$json.business_id}'` : 'null' }},
  '{{ $json.business_token }}',
  '{{ JSON.stringify($json.token_metadata ?? {}) }}'::jsonb,
  '{{ JSON.stringify($json.owner ?? {}) }}'::jsonb,
  '{{ $json.status }}',
  '{{ $json.connected_at }}',
  now()
)
on conflict (waba_id, phone_number_id) do update set
  business_id = excluded.business_id,
  business_token = excluded.business_token,
  token_metadata = excluded.token_metadata,
  owner = excluded.owner,
  status = excluded.status,
  connected_at = excluded.connected_at,
  updated_at = now();

parameters.jsCode: return [{ json: { ok: true, waba_id: $('Build Connected Account Record').item.json.waba_id, phone_number_id: $('Build Connected Account Record').item.json.phone_number_id, status: $('Build Connected Account Record').item.json.status } }];

main.node: HTTP Request: Exchange Code With Meta
HTTP Request: Subscribe WABA To App
